tool_args.label: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2
tool_args.raw_text: Second Avenue Subway Phase 2 will extend from the northern limit of SAS Phase 1 at 105 St, proceed north under Second Ave, and then turn west along 125 St. Three new stations at 106 St, 116 St, and 125 St. Original Forecast Substantial Completion Q3 2032. Budget $6.968B. Serving more than 100,000 riders per day.

payload.description: Will extend Q train service from 96 St to 125 St and Lexington Avenue, with three new ADA-accessible stations at 106 St, 116 St, and 125 St, serving over 100,000 daily riders.

warnings: project.routes_served is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route lists should become project-route relations when supported. Suggested relation: serves_route.

tool_args.label: Interborough Express
tool_args.raw_text: IBX will be a light rail transit system anticipated to have 18 stations over its 14-mile run, end-to-end runtime of approx 32 minutes, 160,000 projected daily riders.

payload.description: Light rail transit system along an existing rail corridor from Sunset Park to Jackson Heights, approximately 14 miles with 18 stations, ~32 minute end-to-end runtime, 160,000 projected daily riders.
Capital Program Committee Meeting from 12:00 - 1:00 PM ET

OMNY capital program reached substantial completion at the end of 2025, achieving both major budgetary and operational milestones, returning more than $60 million to the capital program, and achieving an adoption rate exceeding 98 percent.
MTA Board approved award of Contract 3 (C26203) for 106 St Station cut-and-cover construction to Skanska Walsh Traylor SAS2-C3 Joint Venture.

payload.description: Contract for design and engineering services for three Design-Build contracts for accessibility improvements on Queens Boulevard, Grand Concourse and Nostrand Avenue Lines

payload.claim_text: In contrast to earlier projects such as Phase 1 and Grand Central Madison, which involved dozens of separate contracts, the current approach has streamlined the work into four major contracts, simplifying oversight and execution.
payload.subject: SAS Phase 2

tool_args.label: IBX Jobs Access Increase at Remsen Station

payload.claim_text: currently 141,000 jobs are accessible within a 30-minute commute from the proposed IBX Remsen Station in Brooklyn. When IBX is in operation, 955,000 jobs will be accessible within a 30-minute commute from that location - nearly a seven-fold increase.
payload.subject: IBX Remsen Station

payload.description: Approximately 1,400-foot-long, 65-foot-wide, and 60-foot-deep open-cut excavation across five city blocks along Second Avenue between 105th Street and 110th Street for the 106th Street Station box.
payload.locations: Second Avenue between 105th Street and 110th Street, Manhattan
payload.location_text: between 105th Street and 110th Street along Second Avenue

locations_normalized.raw_text: Second Avenue between 105th Street and 110th Street, Manhattan

normalized_location.raw_text: between 105th Street and 110th Street along Second Avenue

payload.claim_text: The construction area between 105th Street and 110th Street represents the most technically complex portion of the project due to a combination of factors, including the presence of tunnel segments constructed in the 1970s, extensive and densely packed utilities, challenging soil conditions, and a high groundwater table.
payload.subject: SAS Phase 2

payload.claim_text: Software testing for virtual OMNY cards is underway, with beta testing expected in the second quarter and broader rollout planned for the second half of 2026.
payload.subject: OMNY

tool_args.label: IBX Design Change - Street Running Eliminated

payload.claim_text: Street-running has been eliminated from the project in order to reduce travel times and, instead, IBX will travel below Metropolitan Avenue in a tunnel
